2605.30930.title: TUX: Measuring Human--AI Tacit Understanding
2605.30930.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) increasingly act as collaborative partners, human--AI alignment is often evaluated through explicit task success, accuracy, or reward optimization. Yet many collaborative settings depend on tacit understanding: whether an agent can align with a human's evaluative stance or representational priors without clear objectives, communication, or feedback. To study this capacity, we develop a spectrum-placement task inspired by the social party game Wavelength, in which humans and agents independently place concepts along subjective spectra. We operationalize the Tacit Understanding Index (TUX) as a pairwise measure of similarity between human and agent judgments, and evaluate it with 241 human participants and 200 profile-conditioned LLM agents across four models. We find that nearest human--agent pairs in trait space achieve significantly higher TUX, suggesting that tacit alignment is structured by person-level characteristics rather than random similarity. Regression analyses show that TUX becomes more explainable as predictor sets become richer, with individual traits, decision-making styles, and confidence improving over aggregate trait-distance baselines. These findings suggest that tacit understanding between humans and LLMs is measurable, while revealing the limits of profile-based conditioning for capturing deeper representational alignment.
2605.30930.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地作為合作夥伴，人類與人工智慧的對齊通常通過明確的任務成功、準確性或獎勵優化來評估。<lb/>然而，許多合作環境依賴於默契理解：一個代理是否能夠在沒有明確目標、溝通或反饋的情況下，與人類的評價立場或表徵先驗對齊。<lb/>為了研究這一能力，我們開發了一個受社交派對遊戲Wavelength啟發的光譜放置任務，在這個任務中，人類和代理獨立地將概念放置在主觀光譜上。<lb/>我們將默契理解指數（TUX）操作化為人類和代理判斷之間的成對相似性度量，並在四個模型中對241名人類參與者和200名基於個人資料的LLM代理進行評估。<lb/>我們發現，在特徵空間中，最近的人類與代理配對達到了顯著更高的TUX，這表明默契對齊是由個人層面的特徵結構化的，而不是隨機相似性。<lb/>回歸分析顯示，隨著預測變量集變得更加豐富，TUX變得更具可解釋性，個體特徵、決策風格和信心在整體特徵距離基準之上有所改善。<lb/>這些發現表明，人類與LLMs之間的默契理解是可測量的，同時揭示了基於個人資料的條件化在捕捉更深層表徵對齊方面的局限性。
2605.30930.author: Yueshen Li et.al.
2605.30930.authors: Yueshen Li, Hanyi Min, Vedant Das Swain, Koustuv Saha
